task.name: RBTV Help
task.description: Display RBTV commands and deep-dive into any command to understand its workflows
task.objective: Help users discover and understand RBTV commands by dynamically scanning available commands and explaining their underlying workflows.

llm.rule: Read the complete task before starting execution.
No hardcoded information — all command descriptions come from reading files.
Dynamic discovery — if new commands are added, they appear automatically.
Read before explaining — always load the actual workflow file before explaining.
Handle agent files — if the command loads an agent, read the agent file to find its menu items and workflows.

substep.title: Discover Available Commands
substep.action: For each bmad-rbtv-*.md file found, extract from its frontmatter: name (the command name) and description (what it does).
Build a numbered list of available commands.

substep.title: Present Commands
substep.action: Present the commands as a numbered menu with format: [N] /bmad-rbtv-{name} — {description}
Include options: Enter a number to deep-dive, Enter 'X' to exit help.
HALT and WAIT for user selection.

substep.title: Read Command File
substep.action: Load the full .md file for the selected command.

substep.title: Identify Workflow
substep.action: Look for: workflow= attributes, exec= attributes, or references to workflows/ paths.

substep.title: Read and Explain Workflow
substep.action: Load the workflow.md from the referenced path.
Extract and explain: Purpose (from description/goal), Modes (Create/Edit/Validate), Steps (from steps-c/), Outputs (what artifacts it produces), How to use (synthesized usage guide).

substep.title: Present and Return
substep.action: Present the explanation. Offer: Enter another number, 'B' to go back to list, 'X' to exit.
HALT and WAIT for user selection.

halt-conditions.condition: Commands folder is empty — display: "No RBTV commands found. Run install-rbtv.py to sync commands."
Workflow file missing — display: "This command's workflow is not yet available. It may be in development."
Unable to parse — show what was found and ask user if they want to see the raw file.

task.exit: When user enters 'X' or 'exit': Thank them and end the help session. Suggest /bmad-help for full BMAD ecosystem help.
